channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Thanks Olympics. We Needed This.
item.description: The Olympics have brought a much needed injection of awe, a welcome breather from a barrage of disturbing news.

item.title: A Deadly Mass Shooting in Canada, and the Latest on the Search for Nancy Guthrie
item.description: Plus, how extremist groups are using video games to recruit children online.

item.title: Paging Dr. Chatbot
item.description: We look at when you can trust A.I. with your health, and when you can’t.

item.title: From gifting a hat to tossing them onto the rink, a history of hat tricks in sports
item.description: Hat tricks have a rich history in hockey, but it didn't start there. For NPR's Word of the Week, we trace the term's some 150-year-history and why it'...

item.title: Shootings at school and home in British Columbia, Canada, leave 10 dead
item.description: A shooting at a school in British Columbia left seven people dead, while two more were found dead at a nearby home, authorities said. A woman who poli...

item.title: Pam Bondi to face questions from House lawmakers about her helm of the DOJ
item.description: The attorney general's appearance before the House Judiciary Committee comes one year into her tenure, a period marked by a striking departure from tr...

item.title: Trump's EPA plans to end a key climate pollution regulation
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency is eliminating a Clean Air Act finding from 2009 that is the basis for much of the federal government's actions to...

item.title: Person detained in Nancy Guthrie case is released. And, Bondi testifies before House
item.description: Police have released a person taken in for questioning in connection with Nancy Guthrie's disappearance. And, AG Pam Bondi will testify before the Hou...

item.title: Trump to Meet Netanyahu in Washington Amid Tensions With Iran
item.description: It will be the sixth visit by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel to the United States to meet with President Trump since the president began ...

item.title: Canada Launched Major Gun Reforms in 2020 After Its Deadliest Mass Shooting
item.description: The country’s deadliest mass shooting, in Nova Scotia, led to the creation of a comprehensive program after 23 people died, including the attacker.

item.title: ‘I will know every victim,’ says mayor of close-knit Tumbler Ridge.
Revelations in Epstein Files Lead to Resignations and Investigations Around the World
item.description: Disclosures in documents released by the Trump administration have roiled the world, leading to resignations and the threat of legal charges far beyon...

item.title: I Left My Job at OpenAI. Putting Ads on ChatGPT Was the Last Straw.
item.description: Ads on ChatGPT aren’t a bad idea. But they have to be done the right way.

item.title: How Hate Groups Are Using Online Games to Recruit Kids
item.description: Fringe movements are using games and other online platforms to draw growing numbers of children to their causes, new data and dozens of interviews sho...

item.title: NATO Is Expected to Step Up Arctic Security. Here’s Why.
item.description: As Russia displays military might in the Arctic Circle, the Western alliance is preparing a mission to increase its presence in that area.
